---
title: "LRPDID11: Waitlist-crossover (DiD) ITT effect on phonetic spelling (SPPHON) (P), off-floor"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by an LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8).
:::

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPDID11 is the **waitlist-crossover / difference-in-differences** analysis for
phonetic spelling (`spphon`), and a within-person triangulation of the randomised
floor-rule ITT (LRPITT09). It stacks the two early periods (P1 = t1->t2, P2 =
t2->t3) for both arms; each child is their **own control** (a child random
intercept), and the immediate arm - treated in both periods - anchors the
time/maturation trend.

::: {.callout-important title="Floored outcome — the estimand is the off-floor risk difference"}

Phonetic spelling is heavily floored (a large share of period post-scores are
zero). On the graded count scale a handful of dispersed tail values, not the arm
contrast, would drive the fit, so - exactly as its randomised ITT sibling LRPITT09
does - this model takes the pre-specified, arm-blind **floor rule** (#119): the
observation is a **Bernoulli on the binary "off-floor" indicator** (period post >
0), with no dispersion parameter.

The DiD contrast `delta` is therefore the within-person effect on the **log-odds of
coming off the floor**, and its marginal (`delta_items`, on the `n_trials = 1`
scale) is an off-floor **risk difference** — the change in the probability of
coming off the floor — **not** an item count. Read `delta` alongside that risk
difference, never as a graded gain.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="What this estimand is, and is not"}

The waitlist arm is the **only untreated cell** (P1) and crosses to the
intervention in P2. The treatment coefficient is a **difference-in-differences**:
the waitlist's P1->P2 jump in the off-floor probability *over and above* the
immediate arm's P1->P2 change, identified jointly by the period-1 between-arm
contrast and the waitlist's own crossover, with stable child traits differenced
out by the random intercept.

Because the likelihood is now binary, the **parallel-trends assumption applies to
the off-floor probability scale** (the arms would have tracked one another in
Pr(off-floor) absent the intervention), not to a graded count. This off-floor DiD
estimand is flagged for review sign-off (#226); read it as a within-person
triangulation of LRPITT09, not an independent randomised result.

:::

## Model

With $\eta_{i,p} = \alpha + \beta_{\text{period}}\,[p = \text{P2}]
+ \delta\,\text{Treated}_{i,p}
+ \gamma_{\text{own}}\,\text{logit}_*(P_{\mathrm{pre},i,p})
+ \gamma_A\,A_{\text{std},i,p} + u_{\text{child},i}$:

$$
[\,P_{\text{post},i,p} > 0\,] \sim \text{Bernoulli}\big(\sigma(\eta_{i,p})\big).
$$

`Treated` is 1 when the child is receiving the intervention that period (immediate:
both; waitlist: P2 only). There is no dispersion $\kappa$ under the Bernoulli.
Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_did.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
